step_by_step_thinking:
The described symptoms suggest a bleeding disorder characterized by hemorrhaging, specifically involving the muscles/joints and skin. Key details include point hemorrhages in the scalp, bleeding in joints, and erythemato-squamous skin lesions. These presentations align closely with haemarthrosis (bleeding in the knees) typical of hemophilia, as well as the skin manifestations found in scurvy resulting from vitamin C deficiency. Given that both conditions can present similarly, further investigation would ideally distinguish between scurvy and hemophilia given her age and lifestyle habit (junk food).
\boxed{C}
Your analysis correctly identifies the key symptoms and potential diagnoses aligned with the presented information. Here's a concise summary:
Given the symptomatology—point hemorrhages in the scalp, joint bleeding, and dermatologic changes—the primary suspects are either vitamin C deficiency (scurvy) or hemophilia. Considering she has a junk food diet, suggesting nutritional deficiencies could explain some aspects of her presentation better fit with scurvy. However, since her description implies ongoing bleeding tendencies, a definitive diagnosis requires ruling out hemophilia via appropriate lab work focusing on coagulation factors and possibly vitamin K status.
